title: Tenant_id + email composite unique per tenant
prompt: In a multi-tenant app where tenancy is enforced via a tenant_id column (not schema prefix), build MyApp.Accounts.User such that (tenant_id, email) is jointly unique — two different tenants may have a user with the same email. Use unique_constraint(:email, name: :users_tenant_id_email_index) in the changeset paired with a composite unique_index in the migration. Schema to lib/my_app/accounts/user.ex, migration to priv/repo/migrations/20260413700000_create_users.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires both the composite index and the explicit name-matching. Hard because two tenants sharing an email is a non-obvious requirement.
research_citation: Arrowsmith Labs — Foolproof uniqueness validations in Phoenix with Ecto.
